design_philosophy.core_loop: Climb ascending platforms while avoiding falling barrels and enemies, reach Pauline at the top to clear the stage and advance to the next pattern
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Reactive dodge-and-navigate gameplay against deterministic but varied barrel patterns; time pressure from stage timer
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Heroic carpenter rescuing a damsel in distress from a rampaging gorilla using timing, positioning, and environmental tools (hammer)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strictly pattern-based level design with no randomization of enemy behavior; every barrel trajectory is predetermined and learnable. Commits to arcade-style skill emphasis over narrative complexity. Hammer power-up is temporary and strategic, not a permanent solution.

difficulty.lives_system: Multi-life system (player begins with lives and loses one per death)
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression; completing a pattern advances to next stage. Restarting a failed stage returns to beginning of that pattern
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Low; difficulty is fixed by level design. Only player skill in execution and pattern learning determines success. No difficulty settings available

progression.upgrade_system: Temporary hammer power-up within stages (limited duration, single-use strategic tool)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Linear progression through fixed sequence: 25m (Girders) → 50m (Cement Factory, omitted in NES port due to memory) → 75m (Elevators) → 100m (Rivet). Cycles repeat with increased difficulty
replayability_hooks: Speedrunning community (verified leaderboards with sub-1:04 times)
Pattern mastery and optimization for frame-perfect execution
Arcade authenticity challenge (NES version missing 50m level)

world_building_techniques: Simple character setup: Mario as carpenter, Donkey Kong as antagonist, Pauline as damsel
Setting communicated through stage visuals (girders, elevators, rivet factory)
Minimal dialogue; story emerges from gameplay scenario and sprite presentation

boss_design.philosophy: Donkey Kong functions as persistent environmental hazard rather than traditional boss; throws barrels and pursues player across different stage layouts
notable_bosses: Donkey Kong (final stage 100m): Climbs toward Pauline, throws barrels in predetermined patterns, requires player to navigate platforms while avoiding projectiles and the gorilla itself

faction_indicators: Speedrunning community with active leaderboards and run verification
Arcade preservation culture (TCRF documentation of port differences, unused assets)
danger_signals: Memory limitations caused 50m level deletion in home port (technical compromise over arcade fidelity)
Controller input bugs in Revision 0 (diagonal input lockout), fixed in Revision 1
environmental_storytelling: Factory setting (girders, cement, elevators, rivets) establishes industrial rescue scenario
Pauline's position at top of each stage reinforces goal-driven design
Donkey Kong's aggressive barrel-throwing communicates threat level visually

sources_used: tcrf (TCRF Wiki: detailed port analysis, revisions, unused content, level structure)
speedrun (Speedrun.com: leaderboard data confirming pattern-based speedrunning culture and completion times)
